answer:
\boxed{B}  

The study found that laparoscopic-assisted ileocolic resections were successfully performed in patients with abscesses, phlegmons, or recurrent disease, with lower morbidity, shorter hospital stays, and less blood loss compared to open surgery. Conversion rates were low (7–20%), and outcomes were favorable across all groups. Therefore, these factors are not contraindications to the laparoscopic approach.
